overrides.override_summary: Prohibition on restricting franchisee right to join association is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisees in Michigan cannot be prevented from joining franchisee associations

overrides.override_summary: Requirement to assent to release that deprives franchisee of rights is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees retain statutory rights even when signing releases

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause; 30-day cure period required
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan law provides stronger termination protections than the FA alone

overrides.override_summary: Must fairly compensate franchisee at non-renewal (repurchase at FMV) for terms <5 years or if prohibited from competing
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan law requires compensation at non-renewal under certain conditions

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Minneapolis venue provision unenforceable for Michigan franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse transfer without good cause
overrides.why_it_matters: GC must show good cause to withhold transfer consent in Michigan

overrides.override_summary: Minnesota law/forum requirement may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchise law may override choice of law/forum provisions

overrides.override_summary: Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code §§20000-20043 provide franchisee termination, transfer, non-renewal rights
overrides.why_it_matters: California statutory protections override inconsistent FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Post-termination covenant not to compete may be unenforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: California generally prohibits post-employment/post-termination noncompetes

overrides.override_summary: Minneapolis venue/arbitration may be unenforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: California law may void out-of-state forum provisions

overrides.override_summary: Waiver of punitive damages may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may retain punitive damages claims

overrides.override_summary: Cal. Corp. Code §31512 voids waiver of Franchise Investment Law rights. Bus. & Prof. Code §20010 voids waiver of Franchise Relations Act rights
overrides.why_it_matters: General release cannot waive California franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Maximum interest rate is 10% annually (vs. 14% in FA)
overrides.why_it_matters: Late charge rate is capped at 10% in California

overrides.override_summary: No signed acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Anti-fraud protections cannot be waived by contract in California

overrides.override_summary: Hawaii franchise law may override out-of-state forum provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: Minneapolis venue may be unenforceable for Hawaii franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act overrides venue provisions. Litigation/arbitration may be required in Illinois
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees may litigate in Illinois despite FA language

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs franchise relationship for Illinois franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchise law provides additional protections

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act §705/19-20 provides termination and renewal protections
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois law controls termination and non-renewal provisions

overrides.override_summary: General release does not apply to claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees retain Maryland franchise law claims despite release

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims arising under Maryland law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees can litigate in Maryland despite Minneapolis venue requirement

overrides.override_summary: Maryland franchise law provides termination protections including good cause and cure requirements
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland law overrides FA termination provisions where they conflict

overrides.override_summary: Minnesota Franchise Act §80C governs. GC must comply with §80C.14 regarding termination, transfer, and non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Home-state law provides additional protections to Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: NY General Business Law Article 33 (Franchise Sales Act) applies. Choice of law cannot deprive NY franchisees of GBL protections
overrides.why_it_matters: New York franchise law protections override conflicting FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Post-termination covenants not to compete are generally unenforceable in North Dakota
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees are not bound by post-term noncompete

overrides.override_summary: Requiring litigation outside North Dakota is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Minneapolis venue unenforceable for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: General release requirement as condition of renewal/transfer may be void
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees cannot be required to release claims as condition of renewal

overrides.override_summary: RI Franchise Investment Act §19-28.1-14 may require RI venue
overrides.why_it_matters: Rhode Island law may override out-of-state venue provisions

overrides.override_summary: Virginia franchise law may override out-of-state forum provisions under certain conditions
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia franchisees may have right to litigate in Virginia

overrides.override_summary: Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law Ch. 135 provides termination, renewal, and transfer protections
overrides.why_it_matters: Wisconsin law controls termination and non-renewal where it conflicts with FA
